hero.title: Advancing Mobility through Emotion-Aware IoT
hero.summary: Exploring the intersection of Mechanical Engineering, Embedded Machine Learning, and Affective Computing at the University of Aveiro.

bio.title: About Me

image.alt: Portrait of Syed Tahir Ali Shah

paragraphs: Syed Tahir Ali Shah is a multidisciplinary PhD researcher working at the intersection of Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science, and embedded Internet-of-Things (IoT) systems. He is currently a PhD student in Mechanical Engineering at the University of Aveiro (Portugal) and holds a PhD fellowship (BDANA track) supported by Fundação para a Ciência e a Tecnologia (FCT).

With a solid background in Computer Science, his work bridges hardware-software integration, wearable sensing, and edge intelligence. His current research focuses on developing emotion-aware and physiology-aware mobility systems, using data from wearable devices such as heart rate and motion signals together with embedded machine learning models to support safer, more adaptive, and human-centred smart mobility solutions.
Overall, his work promotes the next generation of intelligent systems that respond to human needs in real time, combining engineering design, data science, and embedded computing.
